title: Chinese Vessels Shadow Multinational Maritime Drills in West Philippine Sea During Balikatan Exercises
summary: Warships from the Philippines, US, Japan, Australia, and Canada conducted a multinational maritime exercise in the West Philippine Sea as part of the Balikatan 2026 drills from April 20 to May 1. Chinese vessels remained observed shadowing the exercises from a distance but did not engage in dangerous maneuvers or close proximity. Philippine military officials dismissed the presence as irrelevant to the ongoing activities.
contents:
# Chinese Vessels Shadow Multinational Maritime Drills in West Philippine Sea During Balikatan Exercises

## Overview
The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) announced that 11 warships participated in the Multinational Maritime Event (MME), aimed at enhancing interoperability and operational readiness among partner nations to strengthen regional security and uphold a rules-based international order. Philippine assets included the new guided missile frigates BRP Miguel Malvar and BRP Antonio Luna, along with AW159 and AW109 helicopters, while the Philippine Coast Guard deployed the patrol vessel BRP Cape San Agustin. Balikatan spokesperson Colonel Dennis Hernandez confirmed Chinese vessels remained shadowing the drills but remained at a distance, performing no dangerous actions and having no impact on operations. This event underscores the Philippines' efforts to advance capabilities for safeguarding national sovereignty and maritime interests amid tensions in the West Philippine Sea, particularly relevant to areas like Scarborough Shoal.

## Location
- **Where**: West Philippine Sea, Philippines
- **Country**: Unknown

## Source Attribution
